summary: Matrix recording with very good sound captures a hot first set highlighted by a blazing Truckin' → Smokestack Lightning → High Time stretch, stellar El Paso and a dance‑floor China > Rider.
review: Both reviewers rave about the performance – the first set is described as "very hot" with standout moments like the Truckin'>Smokestack>High Time sequence, an inspired take on El Paso, and a monster China > Rider. The matrix source provides clear, well‑balanced audio that lets the harmonica, vocals and dual‑drummer groove shine through.

ai_rating.rationale: Two detailed reviews are uniformly enthusiastic about both the musical performance (70% weight) and the clear matrix recording (30% weight), yielding an overall rating above average but short of flawless.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Reviewers note a very good sound with clear mix; harmonica and backing vocals are audible, and the dual‑drummer rhythm is well captured. No major dropouts or muffling reported.

show_quality.setlist_flow: First set described as "very hot" with strong opener and seamless transitions, maintaining high energy throughout.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Voice a little ragged but keeps better tabs on vocals; guitar work solid throughout.
band_member_comments.Phil: Bass thumps big like an Aiko, providing a powerful foundation.
band_member_comments.Bob: Cool vocal delivery, draws out words especially on Rider.
band_member_comments.Keys: Brent adds prominent backing vocals and occasional keyboard texture.
band_member_comments.Drums: Two‑drummer setup gives the set a marching‑cadence feel and solid rhythmic drive.
